mnemonics: CLOCK for Consistency: Global states are like a snapshot, but without a global clock, we use logical clocks (Lamport) to order events and avoid time travel paradoxes.
SNAPSHOT for State: A global state is a consistent snapshot of the entire distributed system's state (process states + channel states) at a particular logical time.

CHASM for Chandy-Lamport: Remember the Chandy-Lamport algorithm for taking a consistent global snapshot: a Marker (special message) is the trigger that causes processes to record their state and the state of their incoming channels.
keyPoints: A global state is the combined state of all processes and communication channels in a distributed system.
A consistent global state is one that could have occurred; it avoids 'time travel' by ensuring if an event's effect (receive) is recorded, its cause (send) is also recorded.
The Chandy-Lamport algorithm is a fundamental non-intrusive protocol for recording a consistent global snapshot without stopping the system.
commonMistakes: Assuming a global state is simply the sum of all local states taken at the same real time (ignoring in-transit messages).
Confusing a consistent global state (a possible system state) with an inconsistent one (an impossible state that records a message receive but not its send).
Forgetting that the Chandy-Lamport algorithm records the state of channels (the messages in transit) as well as the state of processes.
